data.title: JX Advanced Metals Sets IPO Price At 820 Yen Per Share Versus 810 Yen-820 Yen Range, Bloomberg Reports
data.description: • ENEOS METALS UNIT JX ADVANCED METALS (5016.T) SETS IPO PRICE AT 820 YEN PER SHARE VERSUS 810 YEN-820 YEN RANGE, BLOOMBERG REPORTS

data.title: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Launches Polyethylene Glycol 3350, Powder for Solution
data.description:
US-based Glenmark Therapeutics, a unit of Glenmark Pharmaceuticals , has launched Polyethylene Glycol 3350, powder for solution, according to a Monday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The powder solution is comparable to the active ingredient in MiraLAX Powder for Solution, 17 grams of Bayer HealthCare.

According to Nielsen syndicated data, MiraLAX powder for solution achieved annual sales of around $555.7 million for the latest 52-week period ending Feb. 22.

Shares of the company were down nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Safra Sarasin Group agrees to buy majority stake in Saxo Bank
data.description: Finland's Mandatum said on Monday it and Geely had agreed to sell their respective 19.8% and 49.9% stakes in Denmark's Saxo Bank to Switzerland's Safra Sarasin Group.

data.title: Morgan Stanley: Partners Group's FY24 Revenue to Meet Market Expectations
data.description:
Partners Group's net revenue for 2024 is expected to rise 7% year over year to 2.08 billion francs, aligning with the Visible Alpha consensus, Morgan Stanley said in an earnings preview note.

The research firm's EBIT estimate stands at 1.28 billion francs, slightly below the consensus of 1.29 billion francs. Meanwhile, net profit is expected to be 1.07 billion francs, in line with market expectations.

"We are ~1% above at the EBITDA level as a result of our in-line cost expectations. We expect the market to focus on commentary around capital markets activity and fundraising environment, transactions pipeline conversion in 2025 and implications for performance fees," analysts wrote.

The Swiss private equity firm's stock is rated equal-weight ahead of its earnings release on Tuesday.

data.title: Mandatum to Sell 20% Saxo Bank Stake for EUR319 Million
data.description:
Mandatum agreed to divest its 19.83% stake in Saxo Bank to Bank J. Safra Sarasin for 319 million euros, according to a Monday release.

The Finnish financial services company is exiting Saxo Bank with Geely Financials Denmark, which owns a 49.88% stake in the Danish investment and trading service provider, also selling its entire shareholding. Mandatum Chair Patrick Lapveteläinen said the transaction is part of the company's plan to exit its non-core assets.

Completion of the transaction is expected before the end of 2025, subject to the approval of relevant regulators, including the Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority and the Danish Financial Supervisory Authority.

data.title: JD.com Sales Likely to Benefit Further From China's Expanded Trade-In Program — Market Talk
data.description: JD.com's earnings are set to benefit further from the government's expanded consumer goods trade-in program, Nomura analysts write in a note. While sales of home appliances have slowed due to front-loaded demand, laptop sales have sustained their momentum, they say. Meanwhile, sales of smartphones could get a boost after the government increased the number of categories eligible for subsidies under the program, the analysts say. Nomura raises its 2025 revenue forecast by 4%, as it now expects JD.com's retail segment to post a 8.3% increase in revenue. The Chinese online retailer's guidance was also higher than expected, it notes. The brokerage maintains a buy rating and raises the target price on ADRs to $55.00 from $50.00. JD.com last closed at $42.29. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Elis Target Raised to EUR27.50 From EUR23.90 by Bernstein
Elis Raised to Outperform From Mkt Perform by Bernstein
Stocks slip, yen gains on Trump trade war, China deflationary woes
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wall Street futures fall after Trump refuses to rule out recession risks
  • Chinese consumer prices decline at fastest pace in 13 months in February
  • US Treasury yields drop, crude oil declines as tariffs sow uncertainty

Wall Street futures sank and the safe-haven yen and Swiss franc strengthened on Monday as building deflationary pressures in China added to growth worries from a lacklustre U.S. economy and an escalating global trade war.

U.S. S&P 500 stock futures pointed 0.5% lower and Nasdaq futures sagged 0.6% as of 0609 GMT.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng slumped 1.8%, and an index of mainland Chinese blue chips (.CSI) eased 0.7%.

Taiwan's equity benchmark TWSE:TAIEX slipped 0.5%, although Japan's Nikkei was 0.4% higher after flipping between small gains and losses.

The yen strengthened some 0.3% to 147.605 per dollar, while the franc firmed 0.2% to 0.8780 per dollar.

European markets offered a bright spot though, with pan-European STOXX 50 futures pointing up 0.55%.

Data on Sunday showed China's consumer price index fell at the sharpest pace in 13 months in February, while producer price deflation extended to a 30th straight month.

Beijing pledged more stimulus to boost consumption and foster innovation in artificial intelligence at the start of the week-long National People's Congress meeting that runs until Tuesday.

Elsewhere, U.S. President Donald Trump in a Fox News interview on Sunday declined to predict whether his tariffs on China, Canada and Mexico would result in a U.S. recession.

A run of soft U.S. economic data continued on Friday after monthly figures showed the labour market created fewer jobs than expected in February, in the first payrolls report capturing Trump's policies.

"I think it's Trump's cavalier approach to economic policy that's rattling sentiment," said Kyle Rodda, senior financial markets analyst at Capital.com.

"Unlike during his first administration, where signs of an economic slowdown or market correction would see a pivot on policy, he is genuinely focused on significant, structural change to the economy - even if it comes at the expense of short-term growth."

U.S. Treasury yields slid, with the 10-year yield dropping as much as 6 basis points to 4.257% and the two-year yield (US2YT=RR) dipping 4.5 bps to 3.956%.

The U.S. dollar index , which measures the currency against six major peers, added 0.1% to 103.82, reversing earlier small losses.

The euro edged up slightly to $1.0839 and sterling was little changed at $1.2917.

In his latest warning to Canada, Trump said on Friday that reciprocal tariffs on dairy and lumber could be imminent.

"For those struggling to keep up, that means that Canadian tariffs were imposed on Tuesday, tweaked on Wednesday, delayed on Thursday, then expanded again on Friday," said Michael Brown, senior research strategist at Pepperstone.

"In that sort of an environment, it is frankly impossible for any market participant to price risk."

The U.S. president also said he is strongly considering sanctions on Russian banks and tariffs on Russian products to try and bring a speedy end to the war in Ukraine.

That has dragged on crude oil, with Brent down 0.5% at $69.99 a barrel and U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude down by 0.6% at $66.63 a barrel.

Cryptocurrency bitcoin lost as much as 7.2% from Friday to reach the lowest this month at $80,085.42, and was last at $82,280.

Optimism about looser regulation and the creation of a cryptocurrency reserve under Trump lifted the token to an all-time high of $109,071.86 in January, but it has struggled since.

The long-awaited executive order on creating the reserve came on Friday, but disappointed many investors by saying there would be no additional buying of bitcoin.

data.title: Besra Gold Shareholder Requests Special Shareholder Meeting
data.description:
Besra Gold said shareholder Quantum Metal Recovery requisitioned a special meeting of the shareholders to vote on the removal of certain directors and the election of new ones, according to a filing with the Australian bourse.

Quantum holds a stake of around 20% in Besra Gold, the filing said.

It proposed that a meeting be held as soon as possible to remove Jon Morda, Michael Higginson, and Chang Loong Lee as directors and elect Matthew Greentree, David Izzard, and William John Blake as directors.

data.title: Multiconsult Bags SBTi Approval for Greenhouse Emmission Reduction Goals
data.description:
Multiconsult said Monday it obtained approval from the Science Based Targets initiative, or SBTi, for its greenhouse gas emission reduction goals.

The Norwegian engineering and consultancy company aims to cut 73% of its absolute scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030, from a 2019 base year, and absolute scope 3 emissions by 30% in the same period.

For the long-term targets, the company plans to slash 90% of its absolute scope 1 and 2 emissions as well as the absolute scope 3 emissions from purchased goods and services, capital goods, fuel- and energy-related activities, and business travel by 2040, from a 2019 base year.

data.title: South Manganese Investment Unit to Sell Manganese Ore to Affiliate
data.description:
South Manganese Investment unit Dameng International Resources agreed to sell 52,000 wet metric tons of Manganese Ore to Guangxi Dameng Manganese Industrial for a little over 43 million yuan, a March 7 filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The manganese ore, which must have a minimum manganese content of 33%, will be from the company's Gabon mine.

Guangxi Dameng Manganese Industrial owns Guinan Dameng International Resources, which holds a 22.64% stake in South Manganese Investment, according to the manganese producer and seller.

data.title: Schneider Electric Gets Board Nod to Invest INR158 Million for Capacity Expansion
data.description:
Schneider Electric Infrastructure has received board approval for a capital expenditure of 158 million Indian rupees, according to a Monday filing with the Indian stock exchanges.

The investment will be used to to increase the existing capacity of ring main units by 25% from 25,600 units to 32,000 units to meet market demand.

The capacity is expected to be completed by the end of June 2026.

Shares of the company were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Palliser urges Rio Tinto to pursue unification of dual-listed structure
data.description:
London-based hedge fund Palliser Capital released on Monday a letter addressed to the chair of global iron ore miner Rio Tinto , , urging further steps toward unifying its dual-listed structure following an appraisal report.

The letter highlighted the findings of an appraisal report, which concluded that the "advantages of unification outweigh the disadvantages" for the company and its shareholders.

Mining giant Rio Tinto currently operates with a dual-listed structure, maintaining separate shareholder bases in the UK (Public Limited Company) and Australia (Limited). As a result, the company is required to hold two annual general meetings: one in London on April 3 and another in Melbourne on May 1.

At these meetings, shareholders will vote on a resolution proposed by Palliser Capital and more than 100 shareholders, urging a review of the current structure, which the fund has described as inefficient and "value destructive."

The company's current listing consists of approximately 371.2 million shares on the Australia Stock Exchange and 1.25 billion shares on the London Stock Exchange.

This move follows similar actions by larger rival BHP Group , which, after facing pressure from activist investors, eliminated its dual-listing structure in 2022 and now primarily lists in Australia.

The upcoming votes at Rio Tinto's annual general meetings will be crucial to determine the future of the company's listing structure.

data.title: Wide Open Agriculture Receives Orders for Protein Isolate Product
data.description:
Wide Open Agriculture said it received initial sales and purchase orders of over 5 metric tonnes of lupin protein isolate from global customers, according to a Monday Australian bourse filing.

It received a purchase order from Tarwi, a lupin-focused health food company in Europe, from a Latin American ingredient distributor, with a focus on Argentina and Chile, as well as one from an Australian food manufacturer.

It also made a sale of lupin protein to a Mexican ingredient distributor.

The firm said the new sales will pave the pathway of expansion into new markets, as it targets opportunities in markets like China, Korea, and Southeast Asia.

Its shares surged 64% on market close on Monday.

data.title: Japan Services Sentiment Falls to 2-1/2-Year Low
data.description:
The gauge for Japan’s service sector sharply declined to 45.6 in February 2025 from 48.6 in the previous month, marking its second consecutive month of decline and the lowest level since August 2022.

The corporate trends index fell within the non-manufacturing industry, while the measure for household budget trends dropped in the retail sector but increased for housing.

Simultaneously, employment saw a rise.

Meanwhile, the economic outlook index slipped to 46.6 in February from 48.0 in January, shaken by concerns over slow economic recovery and persistent cost pressures.

data.title: Computer Age Management, KFin Get Approval for MF Central Joint Venture
data.description:
Computer Age Management Services said the Ministry of Corporate Affairs approved the incorporation of its joint venture with KFin Technologies , on Saturday, according to an Indian bourse filing on Sunday.

The joint venture, named MFC Technologies Private Limited, will develop, maintain, and operate investment management platform MF Central, the filing said.

data.title: Bain Capital, WPP to Break Up, Sell Kantar, FT Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Private-equity group Bain Capital and advertising company WPP plan to break up and sell Kantar, abandoning plans to pursue an initial public offering of the research firm, the Financial Times reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • Bain--which owns 60% of Kantar--and WPP--which keeps a minority stake in the business--had previously considered a listing for what was left of Kantar after a $1 billion sale of the firm's media business to H.I.G. Capital in January, but are now seeking to sell its big divisions due to worries over the IPO market, the FT reports.
  • Bain, WPP and Kantar declined to comment, according to the FT.

Full story: htTps://shorturl.at/R33Wp

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: CBOT soybeans to test support zone of $10.10-1/2 to $10.15-1/4
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean May contract (SK25) is expected to test support zone of $10.10-1/2 to $10.15-1/4 per bushel, a break below which could open the way toward $9.97.

The contract broke support at $10.22-3/4, after failing to break resistance at $10.34-3/4 twice. The next support is at $10.15-1/4. The failure to break $10.34-3/4 suggests both the formation of a top and a completion of the bounce from $9.91.

A break above $10.29-1/2 would indicate the extension of the bounce toward $10.42-1/4 to $10.48-1/4 range. On the daily chart, the contract faces strong resistance around the upper channel line.

The flat move around resistance at $10.23-3/4 signals a dissipation of the bullish momentum. A drop to $10.07-1/2 looks likely.

The downtrend from $10.92-3/4 will be considered to have resumed, once the contract falls to $10.07.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Mosa Industrial Board OKs Private Placement of Up to 30 Million Shares
data.description:
Mosa Industrial's board approved a private placement of up to 30 million shares on Friday to strategic investors and company insiders.

Proceeds will be used for working capital and loan repayments, according to a Friday Malaysian bourse filing.

The issue is subject to shareholders' nod, the manufacturer of high-pressure gas chargers and airbag hybrid inflators said.

data.title: DXY: Dollar Index Logs Worst Week in Over Two Years as Tariff Jitters Stress Traders Out
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dollar retreats against rivals
  • Markets in limbo on tariffs
  • What happens next?

Puzzled currency speculators ditched the dollar as following every tariff decision was more complex than figuring out what to get at Starbucks.

👠 Dollar Is Out of Fashion

  • The US dollar index slumped 3.4% last week, marking its worst weekly performance since November 2022. Traders had enough of trying to figure out or anticipate every new tariff decision so they dumped the greenback and instead filled their pockets with rival major currencies.
  • The buck slipped to 103.45 — a low last seen in early November last year — after President Donald Trump came out with a barrage of tariff threats, slapping one tariff and then walking back another.

✨ Tariffs On, Tariffs Off

  • Where has the dizzying labyrinth of tariffs landed? And is that final? (No, it’s not.) Trump last week declared that there’s simply “no room left” for Canada and Mexico to bargain over a deal or even a delay. That’s a 25% levy. A day later it was no more — a month-long reprieve for carmakers was introduced.
  • Then a day later, Trump suspended the 25% levy on almost all goods from its closest neighbors. To this, Trump said that the “big” wave of tariffs is coming in early April to a bunch of countries, including the European Union. Right now, only China’s 20% tariff remains in place.

🚨 What’s “Trumpcession”

  • The jerks and jolts around who gets slapped with what have sent the dollar in a freefall — to the point where markets have started to chat about a “Trumpcession,” (not something you’d like to have your name on). That is, some traders and investors expect Trump’s punishing policies to drag the American economy into a recession.
  • The uncertainty comes right as the Federal Reserve apparently managed to stick the soft landing — Jay Powell and his clique of central bankers lowered inflation through interest rate cuts while the economy continued to grow without tipping into a downturn.

data.title: Kim Forest Enterprise's CEO Steps Down
data.description:
Kim Forest Enterprise's chief executive officer Daniel Huang stepped down on Friday due to a position adjustment.

He will now serve exclusively as the General Manager of the company's unit Essence AI, according to a Friday Taiwa Exchange filing.

The biotechnology company has not yet named a replacement for the CEO role, it said.

data.title: Brazil's Moreira Salles family launches takeover bid for French bottler Verallia
data.description:
Brazil's Moreira Salles family said on Monday it would launch a takeover bid for all shares of French bottler Verallia that it does not own, at an offer price of 30 euros ($32.51) each.

BW Gestao de Investimentos (BWGI), owned by the Moreira Salles holding company Brasil Warrant Administracao de Bens e Empresas (BWSA), said it expects the initial offer period to close towards the end of the first half of 2025.

($1=0.9228 euros)

data.title: Guotong Pipeline's Chief Financial Officer Resigns; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Xinjiang Guotong Pipeline's chief financial officer, Sui Bing, resigned due to personal reasons, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Saturday.

Hang Yu, the company's director and general manager, will act as CFO until the board of directors elects Sui's successor, the Chinese concrete pipe manufacturer said.

The company's shares jumped more than 6% in recent trade.
